reasoning: The likelihood of regular, active-duty US military personnel physically entering Gaza before July 2025 is extremely low. Early February 2025 comments by President Trump regarding possible US deployment for rebuilding Gaza briefly raised speculation, but he walked those comments back a day later. Currently, the US is implementing its role in the January 2025 ceasefire by utilizing private US security contractors at checkpoints (alongside Egyptian contractors), as Israeli forces withdraw. Crucially, the resolution criteria strictly exclude contractors, Special Operation Forces, advisors, and high-ranking diplomats. This eliminates the most plausible edge cases for early deployment. The historical US aversion to placing "boots on the ground" in Gaza—even during complex logistical operations like the 2024 temporary pier—remains in effect. With the June 2025 deadline fast approaching and peacekeeping/reconstruction currently delegated to regional partners, the UN, and contractors, it is highly unlikely that regular conventional US troops will be deployed to Gaza in the next 3.5 months.
